ExecutionPacket.purpose: Implement the approved module without architectural drift.
ExecutionPacket.contract-excerpt: Planned module contract excerpt goes here.
ExecutionPacket.graph-entry-excerpt: Knowledge graph entry excerpt goes here.

dependency-contract-summaries.dependency-1: Structured logger interface with stable marker fields.

notes.note-1: Workers should only read more context when the packet is insufficient.
Only list graph facts that really changed in the code.

required-trace-assertions.assertion-1: Failure path must not emit BLOCK_PERSIST_RESULT.

wave-follow-up.item-1: Run merged-surface integration check for the logger adapter.

phase-follow-up.item-1: Run the broader regression suite before marking the phase done.

FailurePacket.scenario: Primary success path fails on valid input.
FailurePacket.suggested-next-action: Read the contract, inspect the divergent block, and apply the smallest safe fix.

ExecutionPacket.purpose: Freeze the canonical contract package against the lossless GRACE SSOT so every later control-plane and adapter step consumes the same OpenAPI, schema, WebSocket, webhook, enum, and worker DTO surface.

write-scope-notes.note-1: Repository state is still pre-monorepo, so this step creates the minimal `packages/contracts` tree needed for the frozen public surface.
write-scope-notes.note-2: Do not broaden into `packages/sdk-ts`, `packages/sdk-py`, `apps/api`, `workers/*`, `apps/web`, or compose/bootstrap files in this step.

ExecutionPacket.contract-excerpt:
DevelopmentPlan / M-CONTRACTS: - Purpose: provide the canonical API, WebSocket, webhook, enum, and validation contracts for the entire platform. - Scope: OpenAPI routes, shared schemas, public enums, webhook envelopes, WebSocket envelopes, and internal worker DTOs. - Links: M-CONTRACTS, V-M-CONTRACTS, DF-001, DF-003, DF-007. - Note: this module must be complete before the first large implementation wave.
PublicApiContract + WebSocketContract + InternalWorkerContract: - Create endpoints support optional Idempotency-Key, but retry is intentionally non-idempotent. - If delivery strategy is webhook, a webhook URL is required. - The existing upload endpoint preserves one-file-to-one-job semantics, while combined multi-media transcription uses a separate `/v1/transcription-jobs/combined` endpoint. - WebSocket envelope and webhook envelope carry versioned event fields, but polling remains the authoritative read path. - Internal worker claim, progress, artifact, finalize, and cancel-check DTOs are part of the same frozen contract surface, and claim returns ordered inputs rather than assuming a single source.
ImplementationOrder / Phase-1 + DefinitionOfDone + ExecutionRules: - Phase-1 step-1 converts the migration plan into canonical OpenAPI, JSON schema, WebSocket, and webhook contracts. - Freeze polling, webhook, retry, cancel, idempotency, child reuse, and multi-file upload semantics before any major code wave. - Do not treat webhook delivery or WebSocket as stronger than polling.
ExecutionPacket.graph-entry-excerpt:
KnowledgeGraph / M-CONTRACTS: - Path: packages/contracts - Depends: none - Verification ref: V-M-CONTRACTS - Purpose: own shared OpenAPI, JSON schema, webhook, WebSocket, and enum contracts that every runtime surface depends on. - Public annotations already expected in the graph: - export-openapi - export-websocket-schema - export-webhook-schema - type-JobSnapshot - type-DeliveryStrategy - type-ArtifactResolution - type-WorkerFinalizeRequest - type-SourceSet
Existing CrossLinks: - M-CONTRACTS -> M-API-HTTP relation="defines-public-contracts-for" - M-API-HTTP -> M-CONTRACTS relation="depends-on-contracts-from"

dependency-contract-summaries.dependency-1: No upstream module dependencies. This freeze point is consumed next by M-API-STORAGE, M-API-QUEUE, M-API-JOBS, M-API-EVENTS, M-API-HTTP, and M-WORKER-COMMON.

ExecutionPacket.verification-excerpt: VerificationPlan / TestingStrategy / V-M-CONTRACTS / Gate-Phase-1: - Contract tests must cover OpenAPI validation, JSON schema tests for WebSocket and webhook envelopes, SDK compatibility expectations, and delivery-strategy plus idempotency normalization. - Module check: validate OpenAPI, JSON schemas, and enum surfaces against the frozen design decisions. - Success scenario: polling and webhook create requests share the same canonical job snapshot envelope. - Success scenario: combined transcription create request preserves one-job semantics while reusing the canonical job snapshot envelope. - Failure scenario: invalid delivery strategy or missing webhook URL is rejected deterministically. - Required marker: [Contracts][validateContractSurface][BLOCK_VALIDATE_CONTRACT_SURFACE] - Required assertion: retry remains non-idempotent while create endpoints support Idempotency-Key. - Module readiness target: ready-after-contract-tests. - Phase gate evidence: polling, webhook, retry, cancel, idempotency, and child reuse semantics must be represented in contracts and verification before Phase-2 begins.

step-local-verification.command-2: Contract-specific schema validation covering OpenAPI, shared enums, webhook, WebSocket, and internal worker DTO coherence.
step-local-verification.evidence-1: The same canonical job snapshot and error envelope are reused across per-file upload, combined upload, URL, child-create, cancel, retry, artifact-resolve, WebSocket, and webhook surfaces.
step-local-verification.evidence-2: Wave follow-up remains SDK regeneration only; broader phase follow-up is freezing the contract diff before M-API-STORAGE and M-API-QUEUE start.

expected-graph-delta-fields.field-1: imports: remain none unless the final file layout introduces a shared helper strictly inside `packages/contracts`.
expected-graph-delta-fields.field-2: exports: confirm `export-openapi`, `export-validationSchemas`, `export-websocket-schema`, `export-webhook-schema`, and the shared public types already annotated in the knowledge graph.
expected-graph-delta-fields.field-3: annotations: refine only package-level contract annotations that become public and stable after this freeze.
expected-graph-delta-fields.field-4: CrossLinks: no new module links are expected in this step; adjust only if the real public contract surface materially differs from the current graph entry.

expected-verification-delta-fields.field-1: test-files: add concrete `packages/contracts/tests/*` coverage for OpenAPI, shared schemas, WS, webhook, and worker DTO coherence.
expected-verification-delta-fields.field-2: module-checks: preserve a narrow contract-validation command set and record the exact command that becomes authoritative.
expected-verification-delta-fields.field-3: required-log-markers: preserve `[Contracts][validateContractSurface][BLOCK_VALIDATE_CONTRACT_SURFACE]` for the schema validation path.
expected-verification-delta-fields.field-4: follow-up-checks: keep wave follow-up focused on SDK regeneration and phase follow-up focused on contract freeze before control-plane implementation.

notes.note-1: Start by freezing shared enums, the delivery model, job snapshot envelope, error envelope, and artifact-resolution shape; route-specific schemas should reuse those shapes instead of re-declaring them.
notes.note-2: Model multipart and JSON create flows as semantically aligned after defaulting `delivery.strategy=polling`, but preserve the split between per-file upload and explicit combined upload semantics.
notes.note-3: Include internal worker claim, progress, cancel-check, artifacts, and finalize DTOs in the first freeze because M-WORKER-COMMON depends on those contracts immediately after Phase-2 foundation work starts.
notes.note-4: Keep polling authoritative in every schema and example. WebSocket and webhook are delivery layers, not stronger read models.

ExecutionPacket.purpose: Implement the first control-plane persistence boundary that makes PostgreSQL authoritative, MinIO durable, and artifact resolution plus webhook-delivery state reproducible for later job, events, and HTTP steps.

write-scope-notes.note-1: Keep this step inside storage, migrations, and storage-local tests only.
write-scope-notes.note-2: Do not pull queue publishing, HTTP route logic, or job business rules into the storage package.

ExecutionPacket.contract-excerpt:
DevelopmentPlan / M-API-STORAGE: - Purpose: own PostgreSQL persistence, MinIO access, presigned URL resolution, and durable webhook-delivery records. - Scope: SQL persistence, MinIO object IO, presigned URL generation, source and artifact lifecycle checks, and durable delivery bookkeeping. - Links: M-API-STORAGE, V-M-API-STORAGE, DF-001, DF-003, DF-006, DF-007. - Note: `job_submissions` and `webhook_deliveries` are mandatory early tables, not optional polish.
PersistentModel + StorageContract + MigrationMapping: - Storage step owns `job_submissions`, `sources`, `source_sets`, `source_set_items`, `jobs`, `job_artifacts`, `job_events`, and `webhook_deliveries`. - Enforce canonical rules for multi-file idempotent submissions, source/object-key constraints, ordered source sets, job lineage columns, artifact uniqueness, append-only job events, and durable webhook-delivery retry state. - MinIO buckets are `sources` and `artifacts`; clients never upload directly to MinIO in v1, and presigned download links are generated by the API at read time. - `src/telegram_transcriber_bot/service.py` orchestration metadata is replaced by PostgreSQL, MinIO, and Redis-backed infrastructure in the new control plane.
ImplementationOrder / Phase-2 step-1: - Add PostgreSQL migrations for sources, jobs, job_artifacts, job_events, job_submissions, and webhook_deliveries. - Add MinIO integration and presigned artifact resolution assumptions before queue, jobs, events, and HTTP build on top.
ExecutionPacket.graph-entry-excerpt:
KnowledgeGraph / M-API-STORAGE: - Path: apps/api/internal/storage - Depends: M-CONTRACTS - Verification ref: V-M-API-STORAGE - Purpose: persist source metadata, jobs, artifacts, events, and webhook-delivery records while generating presigned artifact links. - Public annotations already expected in the graph: - export-persistSource - export-resolveArtifact - type-JobSubmission - type-WebhookDelivery
Existing CrossLinks: - M-API-JOBS -> M-API-STORAGE relation="persists-through" - M-API-STORAGE -> M-API-JOBS relation="stores-state-for"

dependency-contract-summaries.dependency-1: Consume the frozen delivery strategy, artifact resolution, job snapshot, error envelope, and enum surfaces. Storage must not re-invent public shapes or drift from canonical enum values.
dependency-contract-summaries.dependency-2: M-API-JOBS and M-API-EVENTS consume this storage surface next. Keep storage public methods narrow and persistence-oriented so the next steps reuse source-set and lineage state instead of embedding duplicate table logic elsewhere.

ExecutionPacket.verification-excerpt: VerificationPlan / TestingStrategy / V-M-API-STORAGE / Gate-Phase-2: - Module check: go test ./... -run ApiStorage - Success scenario: source rows, source sets, jobs, artifacts, events, and webhook deliveries persist with correct lineage and constraints. - Success scenario: combined transcription persistence creates one ordered source set plus ordered source_set_items and one job bound to that source set. - Failure scenario: artifact resolution fails cleanly when object store or DB is unavailable. - Required marker: [ApiStorage][persistJob][BLOCK_PERSIST_JOB_AND_ARTIFACT] - Required assertion: successful artifact uploads create DB rows only after object persistence succeeds. - Wave follow-up: run migration application and rollback smoke checks. - Phase follow-up: validate presigned artifact URLs against a real MinIO container. - Phase gate evidence: DB migrations apply cleanly and the next control-plane steps return canonical envelopes built on this storage layer.

step-local-verification.command-2: Migration application and rollback smoke checks for the Phase-2 schema set.
step-local-verification.evidence-1: All required Phase-2 tables and constraints exist with the lossless PersistentModel semantics preserved, including ordered source sets for combined transcription.
step-local-verification.evidence-2: Presigned artifact resolution stays ephemeral and API-owned; object-store or DB outages surface deterministic storage failures instead of partial DB success.

expected-graph-delta-fields.field-1: imports: M-CONTRACTS only, plus internal storage helpers that stay inside the same module boundary.
expected-graph-delta-fields.field-2: exports: confirm `export-persistSource`, `export-resolveArtifact`, and any stable persistence-facing helpers that become public to M-API-JOBS or M-API-EVENTS.
expected-graph-delta-fields.field-3: annotations: refine only storage-owned public types and functions backed by real code, especially job submission and webhook delivery state.
expected-graph-delta-fields.field-4: CrossLinks: preserve the existing M-API-JOBS storage links; add no new public cross-links unless a real storage-facing dependency becomes explicit in code.

expected-verification-delta-fields.field-1: test-files: add migration, MinIO, and persistence tests under `apps/api/internal/storage/*_test.go` and storage-local fixtures.
expected-verification-delta-fields.field-2: module-checks: preserve `go test ./... -run ApiStorage` and add the concrete migration smoke command chosen by the implementation.
expected-verification-delta-fields.field-3: required-log-markers: preserve `[ApiStorage][persistJob][BLOCK_PERSIST_JOB_AND_ARTIFACT]` for the critical persistence path.
expected-verification-delta-fields.field-4: follow-up-checks: wave follow-up is migration smoke; phase follow-up is real MinIO validation and Phase-2 route checks that depend on this storage layer.

notes.note-1: Create artifact rows only after MinIO object writes succeed.
notes.note-2: Presigned URLs are generated at read time; job snapshots and artifact metadata remain available even when URLs expire.
notes.note-3: YouTube sources may exist as DB rows without MinIO source objects in v1, but uploaded and Telegram sources require object keys.

ExecutionPacket.purpose: Introduce the typed asynq publishing surface for Phase-2 without leaking business semantics into transport concerns.

write-scope-notes.note-1: Keep this step in queue publishing and queue-local tests only.
write-scope-notes.note-2: Do not move job lifecycle rules, storage writes, or worker logic into this package.

ExecutionPacket.contract-excerpt:
DevelopmentPlan / M-API-QUEUE: - Purpose: publish typed asynq tasks and encode queue-level retry and timeout defaults without owning business semantics. - Scope: task type mapping, payload shaping, timeout policy, max retry settings, and SkipRetry classification. - Links: M-API-QUEUE, V-M-API-QUEUE, DF-001, DF-004, DF-005, DF-006. - Note: business-visible retry remains API-owned even though asynq performs transport-level retries.
QueueContract: - Queue names and task types are frozen: - transcription -> job:transcription.run (max-retry 3, timeout 2h) - report -> job:report.run (max-retry 2, timeout 45m) - deep_research -> job:deep_research.run (max-retry 2, timeout 2h) - Minimal payload is `job_id` plus attempt number. Full mutable job metadata must not be placed in asynq payloads. - Deterministic business failures must use SkipRetry, including terminal claim rejection, authoritative cancel, missing parent artifact, invalid request data, and terminal model or pipeline failures.
ImplementationOrder / Phase-2 step-2: - Add Redis queue producer through asynq by job type before job orchestration wires the queue into the business layer.
ExecutionPacket.graph-entry-excerpt:
KnowledgeGraph / M-API-QUEUE: - Path: apps/api/internal/queue - Depends: M-CONTRACTS, M-API-JOBS - Verification ref: V-M-API-QUEUE - Purpose: bridge job creation and background execution through asynq queues and retry policies. - Public annotations already expected in the graph: - export-enqueueJob
Existing CrossLinks: - M-API-JOBS -> M-API-QUEUE relation="enqueues-through" - M-API-QUEUE -> M-API-JOBS relation="accepts-work-from"

dependency-contract-summaries.dependency-1: Consume the frozen job types, statuses, and error semantics so queue payloads remain canonical and transport-only.
dependency-contract-summaries.dependency-2: Implementation order puts queue before jobs. Keep the queue surface narrow and transport-oriented so Phase-2 step-3 can call it without forcing business semantics into the queue layer.

ExecutionPacket.verification-excerpt: VerificationPlan / TestingStrategy / V-M-API-QUEUE / Gate-Phase-2: - Module check: go test ./... -run ApiQueue - Success scenario: jobs enqueue with correct task types, payloads, retries, and timeouts. - Failure scenario: deterministic business failures use SkipRetry rather than repeating indefinitely. - Required marker: [ApiQueue][enqueue][BLOCK_ENQUEUE_JOB] - Required assertion: queue payload must not contain full mutable job metadata. - Wave follow-up: run queue smoke checks against Redis and asynq inspector. - Phase follow-up: verify queue behavior under cancel and dependency outage scenarios. - Phase gate evidence: Phase-2 needs an enqueueable control plane before worker extraction starts.

step-local-verification.command-2: Queue smoke checks against Redis for task type, retry, timeout, and SkipRetry behavior.
step-local-verification.evidence-1: Only `job_id` and attempt metadata enter task payloads; mutable snapshots remain in PostgreSQL and are re-read through storage or jobs logic.
step-local-verification.evidence-2: Deterministic business failures produce SkipRetry rather than blind queue repetition.

expected-graph-delta-fields.field-1: imports: M-CONTRACTS only during the queue step itself; do not introduce direct Redis-facing dependencies into unrelated modules through the queue package.
expected-graph-delta-fields.field-2: exports: confirm the stable enqueue entrypoint and any queue-type constants that become public to M-API-JOBS.
expected-graph-delta-fields.field-3: annotations: refine only transport-facing queue annotations backed by real code.
expected-graph-delta-fields.field-4: CrossLinks: preserve the existing M-API-JOBS and M-API-QUEUE relationship; do not create new public links unless a real public queue facade emerges.

expected-verification-delta-fields.field-1: test-files: add queue payload, retry-policy, and SkipRetry tests under `apps/api/internal/queue/*_test.go`.
expected-verification-delta-fields.field-2: module-checks: preserve `go test ./... -run ApiQueue` and add the concrete Redis queue smoke command chosen by implementation.
expected-verification-delta-fields.field-3: required-log-markers: preserve `[ApiQueue][enqueue][BLOCK_ENQUEUE_JOB]` for the authoritative enqueue path.
expected-verification-delta-fields.field-4: follow-up-checks: wave follow-up is queue smoke against Redis; phase follow-up is cancel and outage behavior once jobs and events are wired.

notes.note-1: Transport retry is not business retry. Retry-as-new-job stays API-owned.
notes.note-2: Queue payloads should be stable enough for workers but intentionally too small to become an alternate source of truth.

ExecutionPacket.purpose: Centralize Phase-2 business invariants so create, child reuse, cancel, retry, and lineage logic stay in one authoritative control-plane module.

write-scope-notes.note-1: Keep this step inside the jobs package and jobs-local tests only.
write-scope-notes.note-2: Do not re-implement storage schema logic, queue transport policy, or WebSocket and webhook delivery machinery inside jobs.

ExecutionPacket.contract-excerpt:
DevelopmentPlan / M-API-JOBS: - Purpose: enforce delivery strategy, idempotency, job lineage, child reuse, cancel, retry, and terminal-state invariants. - Scope: root job creation, child-job creation, state transitions, version increments, retry lineage, cancel semantics, and idempotency enforcement. - Links: M-API-JOBS, V-M-API-JOBS, DF-001, DF-002, DF-004, DF-005, DF-006, DF-007. - Note: workers must never bypass this module's transition rules.
PublicApiContract + RetryPolicy + CancellationPolicy + PersistentModel: - Create endpoints support optional Idempotency-Key; equivalent request plus same key returns the original accepted response. - The existing upload endpoint preserves one-file-to-one-job behavior; combined upload creates one job bound to one ordered source set. - Create-child behavior is stronger than idempotency: if a canonical child already exists, return it even without an idempotency key. - Retry is intentionally non-idempotent and creates a new row linked by `retry_of_job_id`; historical rows stay immutable. - Cancel may finalize queued jobs directly as canceled, or set `cancel_requested` for running jobs; terminal jobs return unchanged. - Persistent model rules freeze root, parent, retry, source-set, delivery, progress, and child uniqueness semantics.
ImplementationOrder / Phase-2 step-3: - Implement job creation, child reuse, cancel, retry, and lineage rules after storage and queue primitives exist but before event and HTTP adapters close the public surface.
ExecutionPacket.graph-entry-excerpt:
KnowledgeGraph / M-API-JOBS: - Path: apps/api/internal/jobs - Depends: M-CONTRACTS, M-API-STORAGE, M-API-QUEUE, M-API-EVENTS - Verification ref: V-M-API-JOBS - Purpose: own job creation, lineage, status transitions, child-job reuse, retry, and cancel semantics. - Public annotations already expected in the graph: - export-createJob - export-transitionJob - type-JobLineage - type-CombinedTranscriptionJob
Existing CrossLinks: - M-API-JOBS -> M-API-STORAGE relation="persists-through" - M-API-JOBS -> M-API-QUEUE relation="enqueues-through" - M-API-JOBS -> M-API-EVENTS relation="emits-through" - M-API-JOBS -> M-API-HTTP relation="served-through"

dependency-contract-summaries.dependency-1: Consume the frozen request, snapshot, error, delivery, cancel, retry, and child-create contract semantics without redefining them in the jobs package.
dependency-contract-summaries.dependency-2: Use storage as the only authoritative read and write path for submissions, sources, source sets, jobs, artifacts, events, and delivery state.
dependency-contract-summaries.dependency-3: Queue publishing must stay transport-only. Jobs decides if and when enqueue happens, but must not leak mutable business state into queue payloads.
dependency-contract-summaries.dependency-4: Knowledge graph marks events as a dependency even though implementation order places jobs first. Keep jobs on a narrow event-emission seam and do not absorb WebSocket or webhook logic here.

ExecutionPacket.verification-excerpt: VerificationPlan / TestingStrategy / V-M-API-JOBS / Gate-Phase-2: - Module check: go test ./... -run ApiJobs - Success scenario: create root and child jobs under correct lineage and delivery rules. - Success scenario: combined transcription create produces one job from an ordered source set without breaking per-file upload semantics. - Failure scenario: reject invalid transitions, malformed idempotency replays, and missing artifact prerequisites. - Required marker: [ApiJobs][transitionJob][BLOCK_VALIDATE_AND_TRANSITION_JOB] - Required assertion: retry must create a new row and never mutate terminal historical rows. - Required assertion: combined transcription create must not fan out into one job per file. - Testing strategy must prove canonical child reuse, retry-as-new-row, and polling remaining authoritative for webhook-configured jobs. - Module readiness target: ready-after-state-transition-tests. - Wave follow-up: exercise cancel and retry against real workers, not only storage mocks. - Phase follow-up: cross-check child reuse and retry lineage in UI and Telegram flows.

step-local-verification.command-2: State-transition and lineage checks covering idempotent create, canonical child reuse, cancel, retry, and required-artifact validation.
step-local-verification.evidence-1: Retry creates a new lineage-linked row, never mutates historical rows, and does not copy artifacts or job events forward.
step-local-verification.evidence-2: Create-child requests reuse canonical children unless explicit retry is invoked, and combined transcription create preserves one-job semantics over ordered inputs.

expected-graph-delta-fields.field-1: imports: M-CONTRACTS, M-API-STORAGE, M-API-QUEUE, and the narrowest possible event-emission seam needed for the next M-API-EVENTS integration step.
expected-graph-delta-fields.field-2: exports: confirm stable create, transition, cancel, retry, and child-job entrypoints that M-API-HTTP will route to.
expected-graph-delta-fields.field-3: annotations: refine only job orchestration functions and types that become stable public module surface.
expected-graph-delta-fields.field-4: CrossLinks: preserve the existing jobs-to-storage, jobs-to-queue, jobs-to-events, and jobs-to-http model; add nothing broader unless code makes a new public dependency explicit.

expected-verification-delta-fields.field-1: test-files: add lineage, idempotency, child reuse, cancel, retry, and transition tests under `apps/api/internal/jobs/*_test.go`.
expected-verification-delta-fields.field-2: module-checks: preserve `go test ./... -run ApiJobs` and record any focused state-transition command added during implementation.
expected-verification-delta-fields.field-3: required-log-markers: preserve `[ApiJobs][transitionJob][BLOCK_VALIDATE_AND_TRANSITION_JOB]` for the authoritative transition path.
expected-verification-delta-fields.field-4: follow-up-checks: wave follow-up is real worker integration; phase follow-up is cross-adapter lineage verification after Phase-5 adapters exist.

notes.note-1: Create-child semantics are stronger than idempotency and should not be reinterpreted as "always create new."
notes.note-2: Workers, adapters, and HTTP handlers must route through jobs rather than mutating job rows directly.
notes.note-3: Do not implement retry by mutating a finished job row.

ExecutionPacket.purpose: Make versioned job events durable and fanout-capable without turning WebSocket or webhook into a stronger source of truth than polling.

write-scope-notes.note-1: Keep this step in event persistence, WebSocket fanout, webhook scheduling, and events-local tests only.
write-scope-notes.note-2: Do not create an alternate read model or route orchestration logic here.

ExecutionPacket.contract-excerpt:
DevelopmentPlan / M-API-EVENTS: - Purpose: emit authoritative job events to job timelines, WebSocket clients, and webhook deliveries. - Scope: versioned event persistence, WebSocket fanout, webhook scheduling, and reconnect-safe event semantics. - Links: M-API-EVENTS, V-M-API-EVENTS, DF-003. - Note: WebSocket is a low-latency UI channel; polling remains the correctness path.
PersistentModel + WebSocketContract + PublicApiContract webhook delivery: - `job_events` is append-only, one row per job version. - `webhook_deliveries` persists bounded retry state across API restarts and references `job_events`, not mutable job rows. - Webhook delivery is best-effort with bounded retry and durable state. - WebSocket clients must reconcile with REST on reconnect or version gap; polling remains authoritative.
ImplementationOrder / Phase-2 step-4: - Implement job events, WebSocket fanout, and persisted webhook delivery scheduling after the jobs module owns authoritative transitions.
ExecutionPacket.graph-entry-excerpt:
KnowledgeGraph / M-API-EVENTS: - Path: apps/api/internal/ws - Depends: M-CONTRACTS, M-API-STORAGE - Verification ref: V-M-API-EVENTS - Purpose: emit timeline, WebSocket, and webhook events from authoritative job transitions. - Public annotations already expected in the graph: - export-broadcastEvent - export-scheduleWebhook - type-JobEventEnvelope
Existing CrossLinks: - M-API-JOBS -> M-API-EVENTS relation="emits-through" - M-API-EVENTS -> M-API-JOBS relation="observes-transitions-from"

dependency-contract-summaries.dependency-1: Consume the canonical WebSocket event envelope, webhook fields, delivery semantics, and polling-truth rule without redefining them inside the events module.
dependency-contract-summaries.dependency-2: Persist append-only event rows and durable webhook-delivery rows through the authoritative storage layer.
dependency-contract-summaries.dependency-3: Observe authoritative transitions from jobs and emit versioned events from those transitions rather than from speculative route or worker state.

ExecutionPacket.verification-excerpt: VerificationPlan / TestingStrategy / V-M-API-EVENTS / Gate-Phase-2: - Module check: go test ./... -run ApiEvents - Success scenario: versioned job events emit to timeline, WebSocket, and webhook delivery persistence. - Failure scenario: webhook retries back off and persist state across restarts until delivered or dead. - Required marker: [ApiEvents][emitJobEvent][BLOCK_EMIT_VERSIONED_EVENT] - Required assertion: polling must remain authoritative even when WebSocket or webhook delivery succeeds. - Module readiness target: caution, because webhook retry and WebSocket consistency are review-heavy until live integration checks exist. - Wave follow-up: simulate network failure and 5xx webhook responses. - Phase follow-up: verify UI reconnect behavior after forced WebSocket disconnect. - Phase gate evidence: WebSocket and webhook delivery persistence are wired from versioned events before Phase-2 closes.

step-local-verification.command-2: Webhook retry and WebSocket reconnect simulations covering network failure, timeout, 5xx, and version-gap recovery.
step-local-verification.evidence-1: One job version maps to one persisted event row, and webhook delivery state survives API restarts.
step-local-verification.evidence-2: WebSocket fast-path behavior never suppresses the need for REST reconciliation on reconnect or version gaps.

expected-graph-delta-fields.field-1: imports: M-CONTRACTS and M-API-STORAGE, plus the narrow jobs-facing event trigger surface actually needed in code.
expected-graph-delta-fields.field-2: exports: confirm stable broadcast and webhook scheduling entrypoints plus the public event-envelope type.
expected-graph-delta-fields.field-3: annotations: refine only event-emission and webhook-delivery annotations that become public module surface.
expected-graph-delta-fields.field-4: CrossLinks: preserve the existing jobs and events relationship; add no new public links unless HTTP or another module directly consumes events through a stable public contract.

expected-verification-delta-fields.field-1: test-files: add timeline, WebSocket, webhook retry, and restart-survival tests under `apps/api/internal/ws/*_test.go`.
expected-verification-delta-fields.field-2: module-checks: preserve `go test ./... -run ApiEvents` and record the concrete webhook-failure and reconnect smoke commands chosen by implementation.
expected-verification-delta-fields.field-3: required-log-markers: preserve `[ApiEvents][emitJobEvent][BLOCK_EMIT_VERSIONED_EVENT]` for the event emission path.
expected-verification-delta-fields.field-4: follow-up-checks: wave follow-up is simulated delivery failure; phase follow-up is UI reconnect verification and broader Phase-2 integration.

notes.note-1: Webhook deliveries reference immutable event rows rather than mutable job rows.
notes.note-2: Do not let WebSocket become a hidden authoritative read model.

ExecutionPacket.purpose: Bind the frozen public and internal HTTP surface to the completed Phase-2 control-plane modules without moving business logic out of jobs, storage, queue, or events.

write-scope-notes.note-1: Keep this step in HTTP handlers, request shaping, WebSocket session entry, and API-local tests only.
write-scope-notes.note-2: Do not move orchestration, persistence, queue, or event logic into the route layer.

ExecutionPacket.contract-excerpt:
DevelopmentPlan / M-API-HTTP: - Purpose: expose public and internal HTTP routes that bind incoming requests to API job orchestration logic. - Scope: multipart upload handling, JSON request validation, route binding, error envelopes, and WebSocket session entry. - Links: M-API-HTTP, V-M-API-HTTP, DF-001, DF-002, DF-003, DF-004, DF-005, DF-006, DF-007. - Note: public routes and internal worker routes should remain explicit rather than merged by convention.
PublicApiContract + InternalWorkerContract + ExecutionRules: - Public routes cover per-file multipart upload, combined multipart upload, URL create, get, list, report child create, deep-research child create, cancel, retry, artifact resolve, and events. - Internal worker routes cover claim, progress, artifacts, finalize, and cancel-check. - Create endpoints support optional Idempotency-Key, delivery strategy, webhook URL validation, and a recommended 1 GiB request limit. - Error envelopes use stable `error.code`, `error.message`, and `error.details`. - Do not merge public client routes with internal worker-control routes, and do not treat WebSocket as the only status mechanism.
ImplementationOrder / Phase-2 step-5: - Expose public and internal worker routes only after storage, queue, jobs, and events surfaces are already in place.
ExecutionPacket.graph-entry-excerpt:
KnowledgeGraph / M-API-HTTP: - Path: apps/api/internal/api - Depends: M-CONTRACTS, M-API-JOBS, M-API-STORAGE, M-API-QUEUE, M-API-EVENTS - Verification ref: V-M-API-HTTP - Purpose: expose create, list, get, cancel, retry, artifact, event, and internal worker endpoints for the control plane. - Public annotations already expected in the graph: - export-registerRoutes - export-validateRequest
Existing CrossLinks: - M-CONTRACTS -> M-API-HTTP relation="defines-public-contracts-for" - M-API-HTTP -> M-CONTRACTS relation="depends-on-contracts-from" - M-API-HTTP -> M-API-JOBS relation="routes-to" - M-API-HTTP -> M-WORKER-TRANSCRIPTION relation="provides-internal-control-plane-to" - M-API-HTTP -> M-WORKER-REPORT relation="provides-internal-control-plane-to" - M-API-HTTP -> M-WORKER-DEEP-RESEARCH relation="provides-internal-control-plane-to"

dependency-contract-summaries.dependency-1: HTTP must shape requests and responses exactly to the frozen public and internal contract surface, including multipart rules, webhook validation, event envelopes, and error codes.
dependency-contract-summaries.dependency-2: Route handlers delegate all business orchestration, lineage, cancel, retry, and child-create rules to jobs.
dependency-contract-summaries.dependency-3: Artifact resolution and job inspection rely on storage-backed authoritative state and presigned-link semantics.
dependency-contract-summaries.dependency-4: WebSocket session entry and event timeline routes rely on the versioned events module while preserving polling as the truth path.
dependency-contract-summaries.dependency-5: Knowledge graph includes queue in the HTTP dependency set. Keep any queue awareness indirect through jobs and do not create direct Redis-facing route logic.

ExecutionPacket.verification-excerpt: VerificationPlan / TestingStrategy / V-M-API-HTTP / Gate-Phase-2: - Module check: go test ./... -run ApiHttp - Success scenario: public endpoints shape accepted requests and responses according to the canonical contract, including the separate combined upload route. - Failure scenario: invalid UUIDs, missing webhook URLs, oversized uploads, and unsupported source URLs return stable error envelopes. - Required marker: [ApiHttp][validateRequest][BLOCK_VALIDATE_REQUEST_AND_SHAPE_RESPONSE] - Required assertion: internal worker endpoints must not be confused with public client endpoints. - Wave follow-up: run API handler tests with real multipart and JSON fixtures for both per-file and combined upload routes. - Phase follow-up: full route regression against generated SDKs and manual curl smoke checks. - Phase gate evidence: core routes return canonical envelopes, and the full Go control plane can create, persist, enqueue, and inspect jobs using contract-compliant routes.

step-local-verification.command-2: Route-level per-file multipart, combined multipart, JSON, WebSocket, and internal worker fixture checks plus targeted manual curl smoke coverage.
step-local-verification.evidence-1: Public route outputs match the canonical job, artifact, event, and error envelopes from M-CONTRACTS.
step-local-verification.evidence-2: Internal worker-control routes stay explicit and do not leak into the public client surface.

expected-graph-delta-fields.field-1: imports: M-CONTRACTS, M-API-JOBS, M-API-STORAGE, and M-API-EVENTS, with any queue awareness remaining indirect through jobs unless code proves otherwise.
expected-graph-delta-fields.field-2: exports: confirm stable route registration, request validation, and WebSocket entrypoints that become public module surface.
expected-graph-delta-fields.field-3: annotations: refine only route-layer exports that stay stable and do not absorb business logic ownership.
expected-graph-delta-fields.field-4: CrossLinks: preserve the current contract, jobs, and worker-facing route graph; add no broader ownership links from HTTP to storage, queue, or events unless real public code requires them.

expected-verification-delta-fields.field-1: test-files: add multipart, JSON, error-envelope, event-route, WebSocket, and internal worker route tests under `apps/api/internal/api/*_test.go`.
expected-verification-delta-fields.field-2: module-checks: preserve `go test ./... -run ApiHttp` and record any fixture-driven handler or curl smoke command added by the implementation.
expected-verification-delta-fields.field-3: required-log-markers: preserve `[ApiHttp][validateRequest][BLOCK_VALIDATE_REQUEST_AND_SHAPE_RESPONSE]` for the route validation path.
expected-verification-delta-fields.field-4: follow-up-checks: wave follow-up is real multipart plus JSON handler coverage; phase follow-up is broader route regression and SDK or manual consumer smoke checks.

notes.note-1: HTTP binds contracts to modules; it does not become the place where business rules live.
notes.note-2: Keep public and internal worker routes explicit and independently testable.

FailurePacket.scenario: Webhook create requests accept `delivery.strategy=webhook` without requiring `delivery.webhook.url`, or return a different job snapshot envelope than polling create flows.
FailurePacket.contract-ref: M-CONTRACTS / V-M-CONTRACTS / TestingStrategy/ContractTests / DF-001 / DF-003 / DF-007

expected-evidence.assertion-1: All create flows default or validate `delivery` consistently and return the same canonical `job` or `jobs` snapshot shape after normalization.
expected-evidence.assertion-2: `POST /v1/jobs/{job_id}/retry` stays non-idempotent and is not merged into create-idempotency rules.

observed-evidence.trace-1: create-transcription-upload schema accepted `delivery_strategy=webhook` with no webhook URL
observed-evidence.trace-2: Webhook or WebSocket envelope drifted away from the canonical job snapshot or delivery shape

FailurePacket.suggested-next-action: Inspect the shared delivery, job snapshot, and event-envelope schemas first, then re-compose route-specific schemas from those shared definitions instead of duplicating branches.

FailurePacket.scenario: Storage writes artifact or delivery rows even though MinIO persistence failed, or Phase-2 tables and constraints do not match the frozen PersistentModel.

expected-evidence.assertion-1: Artifact rows are created only after object upload succeeds.
expected-evidence.assertion-2: `job_submissions`, `job_events`, and `webhook_deliveries` exist with the required uniqueness and retry-state constraints.

observed-evidence.log-1: [ApiStorage][persistJob][BLOCK_PERSIST_JOB_AND_ARTIFACT] emitted before object-store confirmation or migration failure
observed-evidence.trace-1: Database row was inserted while MinIO upload or presigned-link resolution failed
observed-evidence.trace-2: Migration smoke check showed missing Phase-2 tables or constraint drift

FailurePacket.suggested-next-action: Check migration order and object-store transaction boundaries first; restore storage-authoritative sequencing before inspecting downstream jobs or HTTP behavior.

FailurePacket.scenario: Queue payloads start carrying mutable job metadata, or deterministic business failures retry indefinitely instead of using SkipRetry.
FailurePacket.contract-ref: M-API-QUEUE / V-M-API-QUEUE / QueueContract / DF-004 / DF-005 / DF-006

expected-evidence.assertion-1: Queue payload contains only `job_id` plus attempt metadata.
expected-evidence.assertion-2: Terminal claim rejection, authoritative cancel, missing required artifacts, invalid request data, and terminal model failures use SkipRetry.

observed-evidence.trace-1: Task payload serialized mutable job snapshots or delivery metadata
observed-evidence.trace-2: Deterministic business error looped through transport retries instead of terminating

FailurePacket.suggested-next-action: Reduce the payload back to canonical task identity fields and re-check QueueContract ownership rules before touching job orchestration logic.

FailurePacket.scenario: Retry mutates a historical row, canonical child reuse is lost, or cancel and idempotency rules diverge from the frozen PublicApiContract.
FailurePacket.contract-ref: M-API-JOBS / V-M-API-JOBS / PublicApiContract / RetryPolicy / CancellationPolicy / DF-001 / DF-002 / DF-006 / DF-007

expected-evidence.assertion-1: Retry creates a new lineage-linked row and does not copy artifacts or events from the source row.
expected-evidence.assertion-2: Create-child requests reuse the canonical child unless explicit retry is requested.

observed-evidence.trace-1: Retry updated a terminal row in place or mutated historical artifacts
observed-evidence.trace-2: Repeated report or deep-research create request spawned a sibling instead of returning the canonical child

FailurePacket.suggested-next-action: Inspect jobs-level lineage and child-reuse rules first, then confirm storage and queue are still being treated as dependencies rather than sources of business semantics.

FailurePacket.scenario: Webhook delivery state is not durable across restarts, or WebSocket behavior becomes an authoritative state path instead of a low-latency delivery layer.

expected-evidence.assertion-1: One persisted job event maps to one versioned event emission.
expected-evidence.assertion-2: Polling remains authoritative even when WebSocket or webhook delivery succeeds.

observed-evidence.trace-1: Webhook retry state disappeared after restart or retried with non-bounded semantics
observed-evidence.trace-2: WebSocket reconnect path reused stale client state without REST reconciliation

FailurePacket.suggested-next-action: Check append-only event persistence and durable webhook-delivery bookkeeping before touching UI consumers; then restore the polling-truth rule in the event layer.

FailurePacket.scenario: Public and internal worker routes are conflated, or request-validation failures stop returning the canonical contract envelopes.

expected-evidence.assertion-1: Invalid UUIDs, missing webhook URLs, oversized uploads, and unsupported source URLs return stable error envelopes.
expected-evidence.assertion-2: Internal worker claim, progress, artifact, finalize, and cancel-check routes remain explicit and separate from public client endpoints.

observed-evidence.trace-1: Missing webhook URL or multipart validation error returned a non-canonical envelope
observed-evidence.trace-2: Public router exposed internal worker-control paths by convention or reused public request validation in the wrong direction

FailurePacket.suggested-next-action: Restore route-layer separation first, then validate request and response shaping strictly against the frozen M-CONTRACTS surface before inspecting downstream module behavior.
